LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC, CRESCENT HEIGHTS INC, HOWARD S WRIGHT CONSTRUCTION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 ---------- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE TERM "AMENDED" IN PARA. 4 (REALLY PARA. 3) OF THE SETTLEMENT DOCUMENT IS AMBIGUOUS. DEFENDANT ARGUES THAT IT IS A TERM OF ART AND THE EXCLUSION FOR RELEASE ONLY APPLIED TO PENDING LAWSUITS. THE HOA ARGUES THAT THE INTENT OF THE PARTIES WAS TO EXCLUDE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS IN WHATEVER FORM THEY TOOK. DEFENDANTS' TERM OF ART FAILS. THE CONTRACT DEFINES AMENDMENT AS INCLUDING THE FILING OF A CROSS-ACTION, AND A CROSS-ACTION IS NOT TECHNICALLY AN AMENDMENT. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO CORRECT THAT THE HOA LACKED THE AUTHORITY TO RELEASE PLAINTIFF'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS. THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES PERSONAL CLAIMS THAT THE HOA COULD NOT SETTLE PER CC 1368.3. THE COURT SIGNED THREE ORDERS ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

YANG TO SUBMIT 2 OR 3 NAMES TO THE DEFENDANTS BY DECEMBER 30, 2011. LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING IN ITS ENTIRETY. CROSS DEFENDANTS THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, TRAVELERS CASUALTY AND SURETY COMPANY OF AMERICA & CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. SD DEACON IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE WHO MADE THE FRAUDULENT STATEMENTS OR CONCEALED FACTS, THEIR AUTHORITY TO SPEAK, TO WHOM THEY SPOKE, AND WHEN IT WAS SAID OR WRITTEN. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED ON ALL OTHER GROUNDS. THE STIPULATION RE: BINDING ARBITRATION DID NOT DEPRIVE THIS COURT OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION BECAUSE IT DID NOT STAY THIS CASE AND NO PARTY MOVED TO STAY THE CASE PENDING ARBITRATION. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOT BARRED BY CCP 426.30 BECAUSE SD DEACON HAS NOT ASSERTED THE X-C IN "ANY OTHER ACTION" AND SD DEACON DID NOT WAIVE ITS CLAIMS BY FAILING TO ALLEGE THEM AS AN AFFIRMATIVE IN THE ANSWER TO THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S CROSS-COMPLAINT. SD DEACON WAS PERMITTED TO FILE THE CROSS-COMPLAINT UNDER CCP 428.10(B). PER STIPULATION OF PARTIES, THE AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT WILL BE FILED AND SERVED BY MARCH 25. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF CAUSES OF ACTION IN ITS AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF REGARDING DUTY TO DEFEND, DECLARATORY RELIEF REGARDING DUTY TO INDEMNIFY, EXPRESS INDEMNITY AND BREACH OF CONTRACT (AGAINST SD DEACON OF CALIFORNIA, INC.) - MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF REGARDING THE DUTY TO DEFEND AND DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WHICH SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS SOUGHT. REGARDING THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE CONTRACT BETWEEN THE PARTIES DOES NOT EXCLUDE THE DUTY TO DEFEND AND THE CLAIMS ASSERTED ARE EMBRACED BY THE INDEMNITY PROVISIONS IN THE CONTRACT. REGARDING THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF REGARDING THE DUTY TO INDEMNIFY, THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FAILS BECAUSE THERE HAS BEEN NO RECOVERY AGAINST THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION. REGARDING THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FAILS BECAUSE THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES, WHICH IS A REQUIRED ELEMENT OF ITS CONTRACT CLAIMS. A PARTY MAY MOVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF LESS THAN AN ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY IN VERY LIMITED CIRCUMSTANCES, WHICH ARE NOT MET HERE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kristen N. Blake, Esq. of Klinedinst, Tel. 916.444.7573 for cross-defendants and moving party;
Barbara H. Olsen, Esq. of LaMore Brazier riddle & Giampaoli, Tel. 408.280.6800 for cross-complainant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling in its entirety. CROSS DEFENDANTs THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, TRAVELERS CASUALTY AND SURETY COMPANY OF AMERICA & CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer on the grounds that the Cross-Complaint fails to state facts sufficient is sustained with leave to amend. SD Deacon is given leave to amend to allege who made the fraudulent statements or concealed facts, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, and when it was said or written. The demurrer is overruled on all other grounds. The Stipulation re: Binding Arbitration did not deprive this Court of subject matter jurisdiction because it did not stay this case and no party moved to stay the case pending arbitration. The Cross-Complaint is not barred by CCP 426.30 because SD Deacon has not asserted the X-C in "any other action" and SD Deacon did not waive its claims by failing to allege them as an affirmative in the answer to the Metropolitan Association's Cross-Complaint. SD Deacon was permitted to file the Cross-Complaint under CCP 428.10(b). Per stipulation of parties, the amended cross complaint will be filed and served by March 25. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

ATTY. BARBARA H. OLSEN OF LAMORE, BRAZIER, RIDDLE & GIAMPAOLI, TEL. NO. (408) 280-6800 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT SD DEACON OF CALIFORNIA, INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF CAUSES OF ACTION IN ITS AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF REGARDING DUTY TO DEFEND, DECLARATORY RELIEF REGARDING DUTY TO INDEMNIFY, EXPRESS INDEMNITY AND BREACH OF CONTRACT (AGAINST SD DEACON OF CALIFORNIA, INC.) - Motion is Granted as to the seventh cause of action for declaratory relief regarding the duty to defend and denied as to all other causes of action for which summary adjudication is sought. Regarding the seventh cause of action, the contract between the parties does not exclude the duty to defend and the claims asserted are embraced by the indemnity provisions in the contract. Regarding the eighth cause of action for declaratory relief regarding the duty to indemnify, the motion for summary adjudication fails because there has been no recovery against The Metropolitan Association. Regarding the fifth and sixth causes of action, the motion for summary adjudication fails because The Metropolitan Association has not established the amount of damages, which is a required element of its contract claims. A party may move for summary adjudication of less than an entire cause of action only in very limited circumstances, which are not met here. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Off Calendar Sep-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC, CRESCENT HEIGHTS INC, HOWARD S WRIGHT CONSTRUCTION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: David J. Yang of the Law Offices of David J. Yang (982.7315) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; Michael K. Johnson of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (438.6648) for defendant the Metropolitan; David S. Webster of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (925.356.8203) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
----
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied. The term "amended" in para. 4 (really para. 3) of the settlement document is ambiguous. Defendant argues that it is a term of art and the exclusion for release only applied to pending lawsuits. The HOA argues that the intent of the parties was to exclude plaintiff's claims in whatever form they took. Defendants' term of art fails. The contract defines amendment as including the filing of a cross-action, and a cross-action is not technically an amendment. Plaintiff is also correct that the HOA lacked the authority to release plaintiff's individual claims. The 3rd amended complaint alleges personal claims that the HOA could not settle per CC 1368.3. The court signed three orders on defendant's objections.
Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.
